caseStudies.title: Enterprise Knowledge Management
caseStudies.description: AI-powered internal knowledge retrieval for a 20,000-person org.
caseStudies.overview: A global enterprise struggled with institutional knowledge fragmented across wikis, SharePoint sites, and email threads. Employees spent significant time searching for internal documentation, reducing productivity and slowing onboarding for new hires.
caseStudies.problem: No single source of truth existed for internal processes, policies, or technical documentation. Keyword search returned thousands of irrelevant results, and critical institutional knowledge lived exclusively in the heads of long-tenured employees.
caseStudies.solution: A semantic search system was built on top of the existing document repositories, using vector embeddings to understand intent rather than keywords. An internal assistant interface allowed employees to ask natural-language questions and receive direct answers with source citations. Content was automatically re-indexed on a nightly schedule.

results.summary: Knowledge retrieval is now fast and accurate, enabling employees to self-serve on most queries without escalating to subject matter experts. Onboarding programs were redesigned around the new tool.

caseStudies.title: Hybrid Cloud Migration
caseStudies.description: Migrating 400+ legacy workloads to a hybrid cloud architecture.
caseStudies.overview: A financial services firm needed to migrate hundreds of on-premises workloads to a hybrid cloud environment while maintaining strict regulatory compliance, zero downtime, and data residency requirements across multiple jurisdictions.
caseStudies.problem: The existing data center was approaching end-of-life, with hardware refresh costs projected at $40M. Legacy workloads had undocumented dependencies, and the compliance team required detailed audit trails for every data movement across borders.
caseStudies.solution: A phased migration strategy was designed: workloads were catalogued and dependency-mapped, then grouped into migration waves by risk level. Low-risk stateless services moved first, while stateful databases were replicated live before cutover. A compliance automation layer generated audit reports automatically throughout the process.

results.summary: The migration was completed six weeks ahead of schedule with no production incidents. The hybrid architecture gives the firm flexibility to burst to cloud for seasonal peaks while keeping sensitive data on-premises.

caseStudies.title: DevSecOps Transformation
caseStudies.description: Embedding security into the software delivery lifecycle.
caseStudies.overview: Security reviews were a bottleneck in the client's software delivery pipeline, occurring only at release time and routinely delaying deployments. The goal was to shift security left — integrating automated checks throughout the CI/CD pipeline without slowing delivery velocity.
caseStudies.problem: Security vulnerabilities were discovered late in the release cycle, often requiring significant rework. The security team was overwhelmed with manual reviews, and developers had limited visibility into the security implications of their code changes.
caseStudies.solution: SAST, DAST, and dependency scanning tools were integrated directly into the CI pipeline, providing developers with immediate feedback on security issues. A developer security portal was built to track findings, assign ownership, and measure remediation velocity. Security champions were embedded in each product team.

results.summary: Security is now a first-class citizen in the delivery process. Developers report higher confidence in their releases, and the security team has shifted from firefighting to strategic advisory work.
